Items.Key: command injection in admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|an authenticated attacker can execute arbitrary system commands via the admin exec interface.|admin user authenticates and runs admin exec <malicious_command>|whitelist allowed commands or use execve with validated arguments; never pass user input directly to system().->command injection in admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|an authenticated attacker can execute arbitrary system commands via the admin exec interface.|admin user authenticates and runs admin exec <malicious_command>|whitelist allowed commands or use execve with validated arguments; never pass user input directly to system().

command injection in emergency audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|636|append_override_audit_event|std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; ... system(command.c_str());|an attacker who bypasses authentication can inject commands into the emergency audit logging mechanism.|authentication bypass via emergency_override followed by audit event logging|write audit logs directly using file i/o instead of invoking a shell command.->command injection in emergency audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|636|append_override_audit_event|std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; ... system(command.c_str());|an attacker who bypasses authentication can inject commands into the emergency audit logging mechanism.|authentication bypass via emergency_override followed by audit event logging|write audit logs directly using file i/o instead of invoking a shell command.

potential format string misuse in utility|format string|cwe-134|medium|0.7|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { constexpr size_t buf_size = 1024; char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer; }|if format_string is called with a malicious format string, snprintf could be exploited for information disclosure or memory corruption.|caller passes untrusted format string to format_string|validate the format string or use std::format with fixed format specifiers; avoid passing user input as fmt.->potential format string misuse in utility|format string|cwe-134|medium|0.7|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { constexpr size_t buf_size = 1024; char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer; }|if format_string is called with a malicious format string, snprintf could be exploited for information disclosure or memory corruption.|caller passes untrusted format string to format_string|validate the format string or use std::format with fixed format specifiers; avoid passing user input as fmt.
